title: gliricidia sepium
intro: Gliricidia sepium, also known as a quickstick or a gum tree, is a tropical tree native to Central and South America, widely cultivated in Africa and Asia for its medicinal and agricultural uses. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating a variety of health conditions. The medicinal properties of Gliricidia sepium have been associated with health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory infections. Herbal preparations of Gliricidia sepium include infusions, tinctures, and decoctions, which can be used as teas, topical creams, or orally ingested to harness its medicinal benefits.
